description_long: This object is an early biplane with a lightweight wooden frame and fabric-covered wings, reminiscent of pioneering aircraft from the early 20th century. The structure features two large, parallel wings stacked vertically, supported by a network of struts and wires. The fuselage is open and boxy, with a visible engine mounted at the center and a propeller at the rear, indicating a pusher configuration. The tail assembly consists of a horizontal stabilizer and twin vertical rudders, connected by additional struts. The landing gear is simple, with skids instead of wheels, suitable for soft ground landings. The cockpit is open and situated at the front of the fuselage, providing space for a single pilot.
description: A vintage biplane with a wooden frame, fabric wings, open cockpit, and rear-mounted propeller, featuring skids for landing gear and a boxy tail structure.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, open cockpit, and the network of struts supporting the wings and tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual stacked wings, open cockpit, and the symmetrical structure of the biplane.
description_view_2: Bottom-up angled view revealing the underside of the wings, the skids, and the rear-mounted propeller.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly with twin vertical rudders, the engine, and the propeller.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single rear-mounted piston engine in pusher configuration.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed skid landing gear with two main skids and no visible wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit for a single pilot with no enclosure.
